---
title: "A1: What LLMs Actually Do"
subtitle: "Tokens, prediction, and mental models for economists"
---

::: {.callout-warning}
## Sample module — not final content
This file illustrates the intended structure and format for a module. Replace with actual content before publishing.
:::

::: {.module-meta}
[~50 min]{.badge .badge-time}
[Foundations]{.badge .badge-track}
[No coding required]{.badge .badge-no-code}
:::

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this module, students should be able to:

1. <!-- TODO -->
2. <!-- TODO -->
3. <!-- TODO -->

## Overview

<!-- TODO: 1-2 paragraph framing of the key concept -->

## Content

<!-- TODO: Main module content. Suggested sub-sections:
- Core mechanism (e.g., token prediction)
- Mental models: what an LLM is and isn't
- Why it fails (failure modes)
-->

## Exercise

<!-- TODO: Hands-on activity. See exercises/a1/ for worksheet templates. -->

## Discussion Questions

1. <!-- TODO -->
2. <!-- TODO -->
3. <!-- TODO -->

## Key Takeaways

- <!-- TODO -->
- <!-- TODO -->
- <!-- TODO -->

::: {.instructor-note}
**For instructors**: <!-- TODO: Adaptation tips, common questions, demo ideas -->
:::
